mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following three-month FEDFUNDS declines do not significantly exceed zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market deterioration that foreshadows consumer demand contraction and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during any 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate indicate a restrictive monetary stance applied against accelerating inflation, jointly compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following joint increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, accelerating industrial production signals capacity utilization recovery and a hiring inflection, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as operating leverage expands corporate margins.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following INDPRO acceleration do not outperform baseline when UNRATE exceeds 6.0% (rolling 3-year Sharpe ratio below 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth exceeding 1% signals accelerating manufacturing momentum that translates into positive earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising new residential construction permits reflect strengthening household formation and durable goods demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through cyclical earnings leverage.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month HOUST changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising payroll employment and industrial production confirm a broad-based expansion with hiring and output increasing together, reducing recession probability and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The two-way interaction term between PAYEMS and INDPRO changes lacks significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day returns in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth below 1.5% signals demand stagnation that compresses corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the growth scare is priced in rapidly.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following low GDP prints are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
